title: 8 Herbal Teas For Hyperthyroidism
intro_desc: Herbal teas can be a helpful addition to your treatment plan if you have hyperthyroidism. This condition occurs when your thyroid gland produces too much thyroid hormone, causing symptoms like a rapid heartbeat, weight loss, and anxiety. Herbal teas can help alleviate these symptoms by promoting relaxation, reducing inflammation, and supporting thyroid function.  One example is Licorice root (Glycyrrhiza glabra), which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help calm your thyroid gland and reduce its production of thyroid hormone. It also helps to stabilize your blood sugar levels, which can become imbalanced due to hyperthyroidism. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, Licorice root tea can help you feel more calm and centered.  Another example is Ginger tea (Zingiber officinale), which can help to soothe your stomach and reduce nausea, a common symptom of hyperthyroidism. Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can also help to reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland, promoting a more balanced thyroid function. Additionally, Ginger tea can help to warm your body and increase your circulation, making you feel more energized and comfortable.  Astragalus tea (Astragalus membranaceus) is another example of an herbal tea that can help with hyperthyroidism. It has adaptogenic properties that can help your body adapt to stress, reducing the production of thyroid hormone. Astragalus tea can also help to boost your immune system, which can become weakened due to hyperthyroidism. By promoting a balanced thyroid function and immune system, Astragalus tea can help you feel more energetic and resilient.  When you drink herbal teas, you can experience a range of benefits. You may feel more relaxed and calm, with reduced anxiety and a slower heartbeat. You may also experience improved digestion and reduced nausea, making it easier to eat and maintain a healthy weight. Additionally, herbal teas can help to boost your immune system, reducing your risk of infection and promoting overall well-being. By incorporating herbal teas into your daily routine, you can take a natural and holistic approach to managing your hyperthyroidism symptoms.
faq_1: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent hyperthyroidism. Some teas, like those containing ashwagandha, may slow down thyroid production. Others, like those with licorice root, may even reduce thyroid-stimulating hormone. These teas may be beneficial for people with thyroid problems, but their effectiveness is not fully understood and more research is needed.
faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help with hyperthyroidism prevention, but it's not a guarantee. Some herbal teas, like ashwagandha and licorice root, contain compounds that could support thyroid health. However, the effectiveness of these teas can vary from person to person. More research is needed to fully understand their impact on hyperthyroidism.
faq_safe: While herbal teas can be a helpful addition to managing hyperthyroidism symptoms, daily consumption may not be suitable for everyone. Some herbs, like licorice root and motherwort, can interact with thyroid medications or worsen certain conditions. It's essential to monitor your body's response and adjust your tea intake accordingly.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help manage hyperthyroidism symptoms, but the time it takes to see results varies. Some people notice improvements in a few days to a week, while others may take several weeks or even months to feel the effects. It's essential to be patient and consistent with herbal tea consumption.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for hyperthyroidism is best in the morning or early afternoon. This timing helps control symptoms like anxiety and jitters that can occur due to caffeine-like effects of some herbs. Morning tea may also aid digestion and prepare your body for the day ahead, while afternoon tea can provide a mid-day boost.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for hyperthyroidism. Some herbs, like kelp and bladderwrack, have thyroid-stimulating compounds that can increase thyroid hormone levels, making medications less effective. Others, like licorice root and chamomile, can slow down or speed up liver enzymes, affecting how medications are broken down in the body.
faq_pregnant: It's best to limit herbal teas while pregnant, especially if you have hyperthyroidism. Some teas, like those with thyroid-regulating herbs like ashwagandha or bladderwrack, might affect your baby's thyroid development. Stick to mild, pregnancy-safe teas like peppermint or chamomile, but always check the ingredients to be sure.
faq_breastfeeding: When breastfeeding, it's essential to be cautious with herbal teas for hyperthyroidism. Some herbs, like thyroid-boosting ones, can pass into breast milk and affect the baby's thyroid. However, others like peppermint, chamomile, and ginger are generally considered safe. But always check the ingredients and recommended amounts, and consider a lactation-friendly herbal tea specifically designed for breastfeeding mothers.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas contains active constituents like glycyrrhizin, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. Glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce thyroid antibodies, which are often elevated in hyperthyroidism. Flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have antioxidant properties that can help protect the thyroid gland from oxidative stress. The phenolic acids in Glycyrrhiza glabra tea, like ferulic acid, can help inhibit the production of thyroid hormones, which can help regulate thyroid function. The combination of these properties makes Glycyrrhiza glabra tea a potential natural remedy for hyperthyroidism.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal teas for hyperthyroidism
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots.
Clean and chop the roots into small pieces. Use 1 tablespoon of the chopped roots for every 1 cup of water.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot. Add the chopped Glycyrrhiza glabra roots to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea into a cup to remove the roots.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea, 2-3 times a day, after consulting with a doctor or healthcare professional for hyperthyroidism treatment.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale teas contains a compound called gingerol, which has anti-inflammatory properties. Gingerol helps to reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland, which can become swollen and overactive in people with hyperthyroidism. The bioactive constituents in gingerol, such as 6-gingerol and 8-gingerol, also have antioxidant properties that help to neutralize free radicals in the body. These free radicals can contribute to inflammation and oxidative stress, which can worsen hyperthyroidism symptoms. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, gingerol can help to alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism, such as rapid heartbeat and weight loss.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal teas for hyperthyroidism
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 tsp of dried Zingiber officinale root, 1 cup of boiling water, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Measure 1 tsp of dried Zingiber officinale root and place it in the tea infuser or strainer.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the Zingiber officinale root in the tea infuser or strainer.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea into a cup.
Drink 1-2 cups of Zingiber officinale tea per day, 2-3 times a week, under the guidance of a healthcare professional.

remedies.remedy_desc: Astragalus membranaceus teas contains bioactive constituents like saponins, flavonoids, and polysaccharides. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce thyroid hormone production and alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism. The saponins in Astragalus membranaceus tea can block the conversion of T4 to T3, a process that contributes to hyperthyroidism. The flavonoids and polysaccharides in the tea can also help to calm the nervous system and regulate thyroid function. By reducing inflammation and regulating thyroid hormone production, Astragalus membranaceus teas may help to alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism, such as rapid heartbeat and weight loss.
remedies.image_alt: Astragalus membranaceus herbal teas for hyperthyroidism
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1-2 teaspoons of dried Astragalus membranaceus root.
Measure out 1-2 teaspoons of dried Astragalus membranaceus root and place it in a tea infuser or a small muslin bag.
Pour the boiling water over the dried Astragalus membranaceus root and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the Astragalus membranaceus root. You can add honey or lemon to taste.
Drink the Astragalus membranaceus tea 2-3 times a day, ideally 30 minutes before meals, to help manage hyperthyroidism symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng teas contains bioactive constituents like ginsenosides and panaxans that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds help to reduce thyroid hormone production and alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism, such as rapid heartbeat and weight loss. The ginsenosides in Panax ginseng teas also have a regulatory effect on the thyroid gland, helping to normalize thyroid hormone levels. Additionally, the antioxidant properties of Panax ginseng teas help to protect the thyroid gland from oxidative stress, which can contribute to hyperthyroidism. Regular consumption of Panax ginseng teas may help to reduce symptoms of hyperthyroidism and promote a sense of balance and well-being.
remedies.image_alt: Panax ginseng herbal teas for hyperthyroidism
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1 teaspoon of dried Panax ginseng root.
Boil the water in a pot and then reduce the heat to a simmer.
Add the dried Panax ginseng root to the water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the root. You can add honey to taste.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally between meals, for best results.

remedies.remedy_desc: Schisandra chinensis teas contains a unique combination of bioactive constituents, including schisandrins, lignans, and polysaccharides. These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with hyperthyroidism. Schisandrins, in particular, have been shown to inhibit the activity of thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH), which can help regulate thyroid function. The antioxidant properties of Schisandra chinensis teas can also help protect the body from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of thyroid-related problems. By regulating thyroid function and reducing oxidative stress, Schisandra chinensis teas may be a useful natural remedy for managing hyperthyroidism.
remedies.image_alt: Schisandra chinensis herbal teas for hyperthyroidism
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 2 tablespoons of Schisandra chinensis dried roots.
Heat the water in a pot until it reaches boiling point.
Add 2 tablespoons of Schisandra chinensis dried roots to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink 1-2 cups per day as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas contains linalool, linalyl acetate, and camphor, which are active constituents that help alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism. These compounds have a calming effect on the body, reducing anxiety and stress associated with an overactive thyroid gland. Linalool and linalyl acetate also have a sedative effect, which can help regulate the body's metabolism and heart rate. Camphor, on the other hand, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the thyroid gland. By consuming Lavandula angustifolia teas, individuals with hyperthyroidism may experience relief from symptoms such as rapid heartbeat, sweating, and weight loss.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal teas for hyperthyroidism
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Place 2 tablespoons of dried flowers in a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the dried flowers and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate cup.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea, 2-3 times a day, as needed to help manage hyperthyroidism symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis teas contains a group of bioactive constituents called valepotriates and isovaleramide, which have a calming effect on the body and help reduce stress. These constituents also have a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate the symptoms of hyperthyroidism, such as anxiety and restlessness. The valerenic acids in Valeriana officinalis teas have a direct effect on the thyroid gland, reducing the production of thyroid hormones and helping to regulate the body's metabolic rate. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Valeriana officinalis teas can also help to reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland, which can contribute to hyperthyroidism. By reducing thyroid hormone production and inflammation, Valeriana officinalis teas can help to alleviate the symptoms of hyperthyroidism and promote a sense of calm and well-being.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal teas for hyperthyroidism
remedy_procedure: Get 1 cup of boiling water and 1 teaspoon of dried Valeriana officinalis root.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried Valeriana officinalis root and add it to a tea infuser or a small muslin bag.
Pour the boiling water over the Valeriana officinalis root and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea 2-3 times a day as needed, under the guidance of a healthcare professional.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas contains flavonoids, bilobalide, and ginkgolides. These bioactive constituents have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce thyroid hormone production and alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism. The flavonoids in ginkgo biloba tea have been shown to inhibit the conversion of T4 to T3, a process that can contribute to hyperthyroidism. Bilobalide and ginkgolides also have a modulating effect on the thyroid gland, helping to regulate its function and reduce inflammation. By reducing thyroid hormone production and alleviating inflammation, ginkgo biloba tea may help manage symptoms of hyperthyroidism, such as anxiety, tremors, and weight loss.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal teas for hyperthyroidism
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves.
Measure 1 cup of boiling water in a teapot.
Add the Ginkgo biloba leaves to the boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain the leaves.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, after consulting a doctor for proper dosage.
